name: viskores
description: A visualization library for many-threaded devices.
description_good_justification: Succinct descriptions can be found both at the top of the main [README.md](https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/blob/main/README.md) and the project web page (https://viskores.org/).
interact_justification: GitHub issue tracking and GitHub discussions pages are both open for external reporting. CONTRIBUTING.md document describes how external developers can contribute.
contribution_requirements_justification: Coding conventions are documented in the docs/CodingConventions.md file: https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/blob/main/docs/CodingConventions.md
license_location_justification: Non-trivial license location file in repository: <https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/blob/main/LICENSE.txt>.
floss_license_justification: The BSD-3-Clause license is approved by the Open Source Initiative (OSI).
floss_license_osi_justification: The BSD-3-Clause license is approved by the Open Source Initiative (OSI).
documentation_basics_justification: Some documentation basics file contents found.
documentation_interface_justification: Viskores provides a detailed user guide (https://viskores.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) as well as Doxygen-generate documentation (https://viskores.github.io/viskores-doxygen/).
repo_public_justification: Repository on GitHub, which provides public git repositories with URLs.
repo_track_justification: Repository on GitHub, which uses git. git can track the changes, who made them, and when they were made.
repo_interim_justification: The repository's `main` branch is available for use in between versions. Additionally, all official versions are proceeded by one or more "release candidates" that are provided for customers to evaluate before the final release.
repo_distributed_justification: Repository on GitHub, which uses git. git is distributed.
version_unique_justification: Viskores releases follow the standard semantic versioning convention.
release_notes_justification: Release notes are posted on the GitHub releases page (https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/releases). Release notes are also captured within the repository under the docs/changelog/X.X directories (e.g., https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/blob/main/docs/changelog/1.1/release-notes.md).
release_notes_vulns_justification: There have been no known vulnerabilities reported for Viskores.
report_process_justification: Non-trivial SECURITY[.md] file found file in repository: <https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/blob/main/SECURITY.md>. [osps_do_02_01]
report_responses_justification: The majority of bug reports are quickly responded to. As an exception, bug reports raised by a core developer (e.g., @kmorel, @vicentebolea) may not have a response as these are for our internal tracking.
enhancement_responses_justification: Most enhancement requests are submitted in the same issue system as bug reports. Enhancement requests in the discussion page are also responded to.
report_archive_justification: The GitHub issue tracker (https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/issues) makes all past reports and responses available. (Reports for Viskores' predecessor, VTK-m, are also archived at https://gitlab.kitware.com/vtk/vtk-m).
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: There have been no examples of reported vulnerabilities (other than automated checking). SECURITY.md provides an alternate reporting mode for vulnerability responses that are not fast enough.
test_justification: CI tests are automated for every pull requests, and their success is a requirement for merging.
test_invocation_justification: Tests can be invoked with `ctest`, which is typical of projects using CMake for build management.
test_most_justification: Viskores has a comprehensive regression test suite. We believe this test suite covers "most" of the code branches. However, we do not currently have any measurement of code coverage, so we have no direct evidence of this.
test_policy_justification: This is not a formal requirement, but adding regression testing is a common request during review.
tests_are_added_justification:
Here is a list of new features listed in the release notes, their respective PR, and whether there is evidence of testing:

- Support TypeTraits for reference types, #152, **Test Added**
- Enable more types for Clamp function, #149, **Test Added**
- Added CountAllCells and FindAllCells to Cell Locators, #136, **Test Added**
- Added ArrayHandleSOAStride, #124, **Test Added**
- Added template constructor to MaskSelect, #100, No new test
- Extract Arrays to ArrayHandleSOAStride, #126, **Test Added**

Of these 6 new features, 5 had tests explicitly added in the same PR they were created.
tests_documented_added_justification: Adding regression tests is documented in the coding conventions (https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/blob/main/docs/CodingConventions.md).
warnings_justification: Compile warnings when compiling on CI systems are reported.
warnings_strict_justification: Developer builds add additional warnings including `-Wall`.
know_secure_design_justification: Developer @vicentebolea has completed courses offered by OpenSSF.
static_analysis_justification: Scorecard supply-chain security is run weekly by GitHub. (https://github.com/Viskores/viskores/blob/main/.github/workflows/scorecard.yml)
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: All contributed code must correctly pass the regression tests on multiple platforms before being merged. This requirement is (weakly) enforced by GitHub.
discussion_justification: GitHub supports discussions on issues and pull requests.
implementation_languages: C++, CMake, C, Cuda, Python, Dockerfile, Shell
project_entry_attribution: Please credit Kenneth Moreland and the CII Best Practices badge contributors.
